Article.ArticleTitle: Harnessing the power of virtual reality technology to enhance public health genomics skills in Africa.

Abstract.AbstractText: Africa must sustainably bridge the public health genomics skills gap to build a workforce that responds with speed, quality, and scale. Virtual reality offers a novel and cost-effective solution to address this gap, but its implementation must follow best practices.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC), Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. OnyweraD@africacdc.org.
Institute of Infectious Disease and Molecular Medicine, University of Cape Town, Cape Town, South Africa. OnyweraD@africacdc.org.
Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC), Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.
Central Public Health Laboratories (CPHL), Kampala, Uganda.
Genetic Sciences Division, Life Sciences Group, ThermoFisher Scientific, Nairobi, Kenya.
Institut Pasteur de Dakar (IPD), Dakar, Senegal.
Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC), Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Competing interests: The authors declare no competing interests.
